summary:
The PR adds many new completion specs and a safety check for generated items, but introduces a potential runtime error in the SSH known-hosts generator.

The spec adds many new generators and options, but there are a few reliability and correctness issues: an invalid `find` flag, duplicate option definitions, and missing empty‑line filtering in a generator.

comments.comment: The generator accesses `tokens[1]` without verifying that the token exists. If the user hasn't typed a second token yet, `tokens[1]` will be undefined and calling `.endsWith('@')` will throw a TypeError, breaking autocomplete. Guard against missing tokens before using them.
`find` is invoked with `-d 1`, which is not a valid option (should be `-maxdepth 1`). This will cause the generator to fail on most systems. Replace `-d 1` with `-maxdepth 1`.

`servicesGenerator` postProcess splits on newlines but does not filter out empty strings, so a trailing newline will produce a suggestion with an empty name. Add `.filter(line => line.trim() !== '')` before mapping.
`--pinned` option is defined twice in the `list` subcommand (lines 133‑136 and 147‑150). Duplicate option definitions can cause ambiguous behavior. Remove one of the duplicates.
`--pinned` option is also duplicated in the `ls` subcommand (lines 204‑207 and 218‑221). Consolidate to a single definition to avoid conflicts.
